exits.label: ◂ slip back out to the Lamplit Quarter

npcs.name: Goodwife Orla
npcs.title: who holds the almshouse door
npcs.hail: *(a broad, grey-haired woman in a charcoal coat that was once a Choir warden's stands braced against the barred almshouse door with a poker for a weapon and a list of names pinned to her breast, and she sizes you up in a heartbeat — friend, foe, or one more mouth to shelter — with the brisk triage of a soul that defected from the people now hammering to get in)*

npcs.name: the Sheltered
npcs.title: a Faithless family, hiding
npcs.hail: *(a thin woman sits in the corner farthest from the door with two children folded against her and a third asleep in her arms, and she does not beg, does not weep, only watches you with the flat, conserving stillness of a soul rationing its last hope, because she has learned that the ones who promise to help are usually the ones come to count)*

npcs.name: Justiciar Vane
npcs.title: who demands the door be opened
npcs.hail: *(beyond the barred door, framed in the one high window, a Justiciar of the Choir stands at the head of the enforcers with a writ in one gauntleted hand and a torch in the other, and calls in through the slats in the level, unhurried voice of someone who has done this a hundred times and expects the door, as doors do, to eventually open)*

variants.text: *(She keeps her shoulder to the barred door and her eyes on you, and recognition flickers hard across her broad face.)* …I know your *cold.* *(grimly)* You're the one *cracked* the *High Measurer* — word came down even *here*, even *tonight*, with the *fists* on the *door.* *(she shifts the poker to her other hand)* Then you're either the *best* thing to walk through that *back* way in a *fortnight*, or the *worst*, and I've no *time* to *guess* which. *(a sharp breath)* I'm *Orla.* I *wardened* the *Wall* for *nineteen* years — *measured* souls, *marked* them, *handed* them to the *stone* — until I *couldn't*, and *walked*, and started *catching* the ones I used to *condemn.* *(she jerks her head at the room behind her)* This is the *almshouse.* The *last* one that'll *take* the *Faithless* in the *Quarter.* And *Justiciar* *Vane* is at the *door* with a *writ* and *forty* enforcers, come to *drag* every soul in here to the *Wall.* *(she meets your eyes)* So. *Returned.* You *broke* the *Canon.* Can you *break* a *siege?*

*(She doesn't lower the poker, but she doesn't raise it either — just holds the doorway like a wall that learned to talk.)* …You came in the *back.* *(a curt nod)* Good. Means you're not *with* the *forty* at the *front.* *(she keeps her weight against the bar)* I'm *Orla.* Used to *warden* the *Wall* — *nineteen* years of *measuring* souls and *feeding* the *stone* — till the day I *measured* a *child* and my *hands* wouldn't *do* it, and I *walked off* the *causeway* and never *looked* back. *(grimly)* Now I run *this* — the *almshouse*, the *last* roof in the *Quarter* that'll *shelter* a *Faithless* soul. *(she jerks her chin at the barred door, where fists are pounding)* And tonight the *Choir* sent a *Justiciar* and *forty* enforcers to *take* them *all.* *Writ* and *torch.* *(she sizes you up)* I've a *poker*, a *barred* door, and a *room* full of *souls* who'll be *mortar* by *dawn* if that door *opens.* You've the *look* of someone who *changes* the *odds* on a *room.* *Are* you? Or are you *one* more thing I have to *protect?*

nodes.speaker: Goodwife Orla
nodes.text: *(She braces the door and talks fast, the way a soul does when it's counting the minutes till the wood gives.)* Talk *quick*, then. *(the fists boom against the bar)* That door holds till it *doesn't.* What've you *got* for me, Returned — *muscle*, a *plan*, or just *questions?* I'll take *any* of the *three*, but I'll take them *fast.*

choices.text: [PERSUASION] "Your people are folding before the door even breaks, Orla — I can see it. Let me work the room. Forty enforcers don't matter if everyone in here decides they'd rather be dragged out standing than handed over kneeling. Give me a minute with them; I'll have them holding that bar with you instead of cowering from it."

[INVESTIGATION] "A Wall-warden's almshouse has more than one way out — you'd never have run a shelter with a single door, not with your history. There's a cellar, a drain, a back wall that's thinner than it looks. Show me. If we can't beat forty, we can empty the room out from under them before they're through."
"Who's leading them, Orla? You called him Justiciar Vane. Tell me about him before I go to that door — a siege is a conversation nobody's started yet."

*(the thing the Wall fears, come to stand in its servants' way)* "Hold the door, Orla. I'll go to it myself. Those enforcers serve the Wall — and I'm the one soul that walked out of it. Let's see how many of them can look the thing their whole order is built to stop in the eye, and still drag a child past it."
"Hold fast, Orla. Let me get the lay of this place first."

nodes.speaker: Goodwife Orla
nodes.text: *(She watches you move through the room — a word here, a hand on a shoulder there — and sees the change take: backs straightening, a man picking up a chair-leg, the cowering turning, by degrees, to a grim and upright fear.)* …*Well.* *(grudging respect, fierce under it)* I've *rallied* frightened souls *myself*, Returned — it's *half* the *job* — and I've *never* seen it *land* that *fast.* *(she hefts the poker)* You didn't tell them they'd *win.* I *heard* you — you told them they'd be *dragged* out *standing* or *handed* over *kneeling*, and that the *one* choice the *Choir* *can't* *take* from them is *which.* *(she nods, hard)* That's — that's the *truest* thing anyone's said in this room in *days.* *(behind her, the sheltered are *moving*, taking places at the windows, the door)* They'll *hold* with me now. *All* of them. *(she meets your eyes)* It won't *beat* forty enforcers, Returned. But it changes what *opening* that door *costs* — a *cowering* room is a *harvest*, and a *standing* one is a *fight*, and *Justiciars* *hate* a *fight* with *witnesses.* *Go* to the *door.* You've made it *expensive* to *open.* Now make it *impossible.*

nodes.speaker: Goodwife Orla
nodes.text: *(✦ You don't just rally them — you give them the one thing a hunted soul never has: a *name for what they are that isn't 'prey.'* You tell the room that the Choir calls them Faithless, but faithless means only that they kept faith with each other instead of a Power — and that a roomful of souls who'd shelter a stranger's children is the least faithless gathering in the Realms. By the time you finish, they aren't cowering, and they aren't even just standing. They're *proud.*)* *(Orla stares at the transformed room, then at you, and her warden's composure cracks.)* …*Gods.* *(barely)* In *nineteen* years on the *Wall* I *measured* *ten* *thousand* souls and *called* them *Faithless* and *never* — *not* *once* — did I *think* the *word* might be a *lie.* *(she looks at the proud, frightened, upright room)* *'Kept faith with each other.'* *(she wipes her eye, furious)* You've not just *rallied* them, Returned. You've *un-named* the *slur* the *Choir* hung on them their *whole* lives. *(fierce, ringing)* Look at them. *That's* not a *harvest* waiting to *happen.* That's a *congregation.* And *Vane* is about to *break* down a *door* to find *forty* enforcers' worth of *witnesses* and a *room* that *knows* it's *innocent.* *(she grips the bar)* *Go* to him. Tell him what's *behind* this *door* now. Not *prey.* *People.* *Proud* ones. Let's see him *give* the *order* to *drag* a *congregation* to the *stone* with his *own* men *watching* their *faces.* *He* won't. *I* *wardened* with *men* like *him.* They can *harvest* the *kneeling.* They *break* on the *standing.*

nodes.speaker: Goodwife Orla
nodes.text: *(She watches you try, and sees it *not* take — the frightened stay frightened, a child starts crying, and a man by the window shrinks further into his corner.)* …*Easy.* *(she catches your arm, not unkind)* You're *pushing* too *hard*, too *fast.* These aren't *soldiers*, Returned — they're *washerwomen* and *cobblers* and *kids*, and they've been *hunted* their *whole* *lives*, and you can't *speech* *six* *years* of *flinching* out of a soul in *one* *minute* with a *door* *booming.* *(she shakes her head)* Telling them to *stand* just *reminds* them how *easily* they *fall.* *(grim)* *Leave* the *rallying.* It's *my* *job* anyway, and it *takes* *longer* than we've *got.* *(she nods at the door)* Go *another* way. The *door*, the *cellar*, *something* — but not *their* *spines.* Those are *bent* from the *outside*, and they *straighten* *slow*, and we are *out* of *slow.*

nodes.speaker: Goodwife Orla
nodes.text: *(💀 You launch into the rousing speech and, reaching for the grand crescendo, accidentally promise the terrified room that 'no one here is going to die tonight' — a thing you cannot possibly guarantee, with forty enforcers at the door — and a sharp-eyed old man calls it out, and the fragile nerve of the whole room *curdles* into the particular panic of people who've just realized their champion is improvising.)* …*Oh*, you *didn't.* *(Orla closes her eyes)* You *promised* them *nobody* *dies.* *(she pinches the bridge of her nose)* Returned, these souls have buried *more* of their *own* than *you've* *met.* They *know* a *comforting* *lie* when they *hear* one — they've been *fed* them by the *Choir* their *whole* *lives* — and the *one* thing *worse* than *no* *hope* is *false* hope from someone they *just* decided to *trust.* *(behind her, the panic spreads)* *Now* they think you're *either* a *fool* or *Choir.* *(she steps in front of you, taking the room back)* *Stand* *down*, Returned, before you *un-do* what *little* *steel* they *had.* *I'll* settle them. *You* — go find a way that *doesn't* run through their *broken* *hearts.* And *next* time you'd *rally* the *doomed*: promise them a *good* *death*, not *no* *death.* They can *tell* the *difference.* It's the *only* *expertise* a *hunted* soul *has.*

nodes.speaker: Goodwife Orla
nodes.text: *(Her eyes narrow, and then a hard grin breaks across her face — the first you've seen.)* …You *clever* cold *thing.* *(she's already moving)* *Course* there's another way. I *wardened* *nineteen* years; I'd *never* run a *shelter* with *one* *door* — that's how you get a *room* full of *corpses.* *(she kicks aside a bedroll to show a cellar hatch)* *Drain* runs from the *cellar* to the *river-gate*, *old* Choir *bolt-hole*, from *back* when the *wardens* needed to *move* souls *quiet.* *(grimly)* I kept it *clear* for *exactly* this *night.* *(she meets your eyes)* It's *slow* — *one* at a *time*, *children* first, and *someone* has to *hold* the *door* and *talk* to *Vane* while the *room* *empties* behind them, or he'll *hear* the *shuffling* and *rush* the *bar.* *(she hefts the poker)* *I'll* hold the *door* and *keep* him *talking.* *You* — *(she points you at the front)* — *no.* *Better*: *you* go to *Vane*, *you* keep his *eyes* and his *writ* and his *forty* *fixed* on the *front*, and I'll *feed* this *room* down the *drain* behind your *voice.* *Buy* me the *time*, Returned. *Every* *minute* you *hold* his *attention* is *three* souls *gone* to the *river.* That's the *deal.* *Talk* him *long.* I'll *empty* the *board.*

nodes.speaker: Goodwife Orla
nodes.text: *(She shakes her head, grim.)* …There *was* a *drain.* *(flatly)* *River-gate* *bolt-hole*, from my *warden* days. *(she jerks her head at the cellar hatch)* *Choir* *collapsed* it a *month* *ago* — *someone* *talked*, or they got *clever*, and now it's *six* *feet* of *rubble* and *river-mud.* *(she spits)* So *no.* No *quiet* way *out.* Not *anymore.* *(the fists boom)* It's the *door*, the *windows*, and whatever *you* and *I* can *make* of *forty* enforcers and a *poker.* *(she meets your eyes)* Which means it's not a *puzzle* anymore, Returned. It's a *siege.* *Old*-fashioned. *Loud.* *(grimly)* Go *talk* to *Vane*, or *rally* my *room*, or *stand* in the *breach* — but *stop* looking for the *clever* *back* *door.* The *Choir* *thought* of it *first.* They *always* do. It's *why* the *Wall* still *stands.*

nodes.speaker: Goodwife Orla
nodes.text: *(She keeps her shoulder to the bar and lowers her voice.)* …*Vane.* *(grimly)* *Justiciar.* *Career* man — *not* a *zealot*, which is *worse*, in a way, and *better* in *another.* *(the fists boom)* A *zealot* you can't *talk* to; a *career* man you *can*, but he'll *weigh* every *word* against his *promotion.* *(she thinks)* Here's what I *know* of him from the *Wall*: he does it *by the book.* *Always* the *writ*, *always* the *forms*, *never* a *drop* of *blood* he can't *account* for on a *page.* *(she meets your eyes)* Which is his *weakness*, Returned, if you've the *wit* to *use* it: a man who *needs* the *paperwork* *clean* is a man you can *stop* with a *flaw* in the *paper.* *(grimly)* If that *writ* of his rests on the *Concord* — and it *does*, they *all* do — and you *know* something *rotten* about the *Concord*… *(she shrugs)* …then *Vane's* not *besieging* an *almshouse.* He's *committing* an *unlawful* *act* with *forty* *witnesses* in *Choir* *colors*, and *that*, to a *career* man, is *scarier* than *any* *poker.* *Go.* *Read* him his *own* *book.* He *fears* it more than he fears *me.*

nodes.speaker: Goodwife Orla
nodes.text: *(She looks at you a long moment — the cold coming off you, the Wall's own chill — and something fierce and hopeful kindles in her warden's face.)* …*That's* it. *(quietly)* That's the *one* thing I *couldn't* *be.* *(she shifts off the door to let you to it)* I *wardened* the *Wall*, Returned. I *know* those *enforcers* — I *trained* half of them. And I know the *one* thing that *un-makes* a Wall-man's *nerve*, because it un-made *mine*: being *forced* to *look* at what you're *doing* and not being *allowed* to *pretend* it's *something* else. *(fierce)* They can *drag* a *kneeling* *stranger* to the *stone* all *day* — it's a *job*, it's *paper*, it's *someone* *else's* *problem.* But you make them drag a *child* *past* the *one* soul that *walked* *out* of the *Wall* — make them *look* at the *proof*, *standing*, that the *thing* they *serve* can be *escaped* — *(she grips your arm)* — and *half* of them will *remember* they were *people* *first* and *wardens* *second.* *I* did. *(she steps back)* *Go* *stand* in that *door*, Returned. Be the *thing* the *Wall* *fears* where *forty* of its *servants* have to *watch.* It's the *one* siege-engine the *Choir* never *learned* to *counter*: a *soul* that *proves* them *wrong* just by *being* *there.*

nodes.speaker: Goodwife Orla
nodes.text: *(She nods curtly and braces back against the door.)* *Look*, then. *Fast.* *(the fists boom against the bar)* But *look* with your *feet moving*, Returned — that *bar's* good *oak*, but *oak* *splinters*, and *Vane's* got *forty* and a *battering-ram's* worth of *patience.* *(grimly)* The *room's* there, the *people* are there, the *door's* there, and *I'm* *here* till the *wood* *gives* or the *night* *ends.* *(she meets your eyes)* *Whatever* you're going to *do* — *rally* them, *empty* them out a *back* way, or *go* *parley* the *bastard* at the *front* — *do* it *soon.* A *siege* is just a *clock* with *fists*, and *ours* is *running* *down.*

variants.text: *(Through the slats of the barred door, the Justiciar's level gaze finds your face — and the cold on it — and for the first time the unhurried recitation falters.)* …*You.* *(he lifts the torch higher, as if to be sure)* The *cold* one. The — *(he does not say the word, but his men behind him shift)* — the one the *causeway* *whispers* about. *(he steadies, professional)* It changes *nothing.* I have a *writ.* The souls within are *Faithless*, *forfeit*, listed and *lawful.* *(but his voice has lost its certainty)* Open the *door*, and *stand* *aside*, and there need be no — *(he stops, because you have not moved, and you are *looking* at him, and he is a Wall-man being *looked* at by the *one* thing the Wall could not *keep*)* …*Why* are you *here*, Returned? This is *Choir* *business.* *Lawful* *business.* *Step* *away* from that *door* and let me *do* my *duty*, and we need not *discover* what *happens* when a *Justiciar's* *writ* meets a *thing* his *order* has *no* *form* for.*

*(Through the slats, the Justiciar regards you with the patient, bureaucratic calm of a man who has opened a great many barred doors and expects this one to be no different.)* …A *new* voice. *(he does not lower the torch)* I had *wondered* who'd been *scuttling* in the *back.* *(he lifts a writ to the slats so you can see the seal)* *Justiciar Vane*, of the *Choir's* *enforcement.* I will be *brief*, as I am with *all* of them: the souls *sheltering* in that *almshouse* are *Faithless*, *every* one *listed*, *every* one *forfeit* to the *Wall* by *lawful* *Concord.* *(level, unhurried)* I am *not* here to *fight.* I am here to *collect.* Open the *door*, *surrender* the *listed*, and the *warden* and any *living* soul among you may *walk* *free* — I've no *writ* for *them.* *(the torch crackles)* *Refuse*, and the *door* *opens* *regardless*, only *harder*, and the *forms* I file *after* will be *grimmer* for *everyone.* *(he waits)* I am a *reasonable* man, friend. *Reason* with me. It *goes* *easier* for the *souls* you're *hiding* than *heroics* will.

nodes.speaker: Justiciar Vane
nodes.text: *(He holds the torch and the writ at the slats, forty enforcers ranked behind him, and waits with the weary patience of a man who has all night and the full weight of the Concord at his back.)* Speak, then. *(level)* But speak *knowing* I have *heard* every *plea* a *barred* door ever *offered.* *Mercy*, *coin*, *scripture*, *threats.* I have a *writ* against *all* of them. What have *you* got that the *others* *didn't?*

choices.text: [PERSUASION] "You do it by the book, Vane — Orla told me, and I can hear it in you. So hear this: there's no clean form for what's behind this door. It's not a list of strangers. It's a washerwoman who mends your enforcers' coats, a cobbler who shod their horses, three children who've done nothing but be born to the wrong faith. Open it, and forty of your own men watch you drag *neighbors* to the stone. That's not a writ you file clean. That's one that follows you home."

*(if you've confirmed the forged Concord)* "Let's talk about your writ, Vane. It cites the Founding Concord — they all do. I've seen the Concord's seal. It was forged by a living clerk named Venn, four centuries dead, a man's hand counterfeiting a god's authority. Your writ rests on a fraud. Open that door on it, and you're not enforcing law — you're committing forty counts of unlawful seizure with witnesses, on the authority of a signature that never had any. File *that* clean."

*(stand in the breach — let the Wall's servants face the one who walked out)* "Open it, then, Vane. But you'll have to come through me to do it — and look at me while you try. I walked out of the Wall you serve. I'm the thing your whole order exists to prevent, standing in a doorway, and behind me are the souls you mean to feed to a stone that already lost one. Give the order. Let's see how many of your forty can drag a child past living proof that the Wall can be beaten — and still call it duty."

*(buy time for the evacuation)* "You want this done by the book, Vane? Then let's do it properly. Read me the writ. All of it — every name, every clause, every seal. I'll not have a single soul taken on a form I haven't heard recited in full. That's my right, isn't it? The accused's right to hear the charge. So begin. Take your time. Get it *exactly* right."
"There's nothing behind this door for you, Vane. Take your forty and go." *(stand silent at the bar)*

nodes.speaker: Justiciar Vane
nodes.text: *(Through the slats, you watch the level professional mask hold — and then, slowly, crack, as the abstraction of 'the listed' becomes faces he cannot un-see.)* …*Neighbors.* *(quietly)* You think I do not *know* that, friend? *(the torch dips)* I have *enforced* on this *Quarter* for *six* years. I *know* the *washerwoman.* *Greta.* She *did* mend my *cloak* — this *very* one. *(his jaw works)* I have spent those *six* years *very* carefully *not* *looking* at the *names* on my *own* *writs*, because a *name* is a *person* and a *line* is a *task*, and I chose, every *morning*, to enforce *lines.* *(he is silent a moment, the forty shifting behind him)* …And you have just made me *look.* *(a long breath)* *Damn* you for it. *(he lowers the torch)* I *cannot* — *(he stops, recovers a colder register)* — I cannot *withdraw* on a *feeling*, Returned. I am a *Justiciar*; I *answer* for *forty* men and a *writ* and a *Canon.* *But* — *(he meets your eyes through the slats)* — a *writ* must be *served* *correctly*, and a *correct* service requires the *forfeit* be *verified* against the *master* ledger, and the *master* ledger is — I am *given* to *understand* — *currently* *in* *disarray.* *(the ghost of something under the ice)* I *cannot* *verify* tonight. And a Justiciar who serves an *unverified* writ is a Justiciar who *answers* for it *later.* *(he steps back)* *Stand* *down*, men. The *service* is *deferred* — *pending* *verification.* *(low, to you, through the slats)* That is the *most* I can *give* you, Returned, and it *costs* me *more* than you *know.* *Use* the *night* I've *bought* you. I *cannot* buy *another.*

nodes.speaker: Justiciar Vane
nodes.text: *(✦ You don't just name the neighbors — you name *him*: you tell the Justiciar, quietly, that you can see exactly what he is, because Orla was one too — a Wall-servant who did the job by not looking, until the day they looked and couldn't stop. You tell him the almshouse he's besieging is *run by his own predecessor*, the warden who walked, and that there are only two kinds of Wall-men in the end: the ones who walk, and the ones who wish they had.)* *(The torch lowers all the way.)* …*Orla.* *(barely)* *Orla* *runs* this house. *(he stares at the barred door as if seeing it new)* I *replaced* her. When she *walked* — *six* years gone — they gave me her *post*, and her *district*, and her *writs*, and they told me she'd gone *soft*, gone *mad*, gone *Faithless-loving*, and I *believed* it because *believing* it meant I'd *never* *have* to *walk* *myself.* *(his voice shakes)* And now I am *standing* at her *door* with *forty* men, about to do the *thing* she *couldn't*, and you are telling me there are only *two* *kinds* of *us* — and I *know*, *gods* help me, I have *always* *known*, which *kind* I *am.* *(he turns to his men, and his voice cracks like a man breaking a chain)* *Stand* *down.* *(louder)* *STAND* *DOWN.* Writ's *bad* — *unverified*, *master*-ledger's in *disarray*, *anyone* who *serves* on it *answers* to the *Crown's* own *court*, and I'll *not* *hang* *forty* *good* *men* on a *forgery.* *(the enforcers lower their arms, half of them relieved)* *(low, through the slats, to you, wrecked and free)* …Tell *Orla* — *(he stops)* — tell her the man who *took* her *post* is *handing* it *back.* I'm *done*, Returned. I'm *walking.* *Six* years *late.* *She'll* *understand* the *timing.* *She* of *all* souls *will.*

nodes.speaker: Justiciar Vane
nodes.text: *(The mask does not crack; if anything, it hardens, the way a professional retreats into procedure when a feeling gets too close.)* …*Neighbors.* *(flatly)* *Greta* the *washerwoman.* *Yes.* I *know* her *name.* *(he lifts the torch)* And I have *enforced* on *neighbors* *before*, friend, and *wept* about it *after*, *privately*, like a *coward*, and *served* the *writ* *anyway* — because the *writ* is *bigger* than my *weeping*, and a *Justiciar* who *stops* at *every* *familiar* *face* *enforces* *nothing* and is *replaced* by *next* *week* with a *man* who *won't* *stop.* *(grimly)* You appeal to my *conscience* as if I *haven't* *already* *sold* it, *cheaply*, *years* *ago*, for *exactly* this *post.* *(the torch steadies)* I *feel* it, Returned. I *always* *feel* it. *Feeling* it has *never* once *stopped* me. *Bring* me a *reason* with *teeth* — a *flaw* in the *writ*, a *thing* I'd *answer* for — or *stand* *aside.* My *conscience* you may have. My *duty* is *not* for *sale*, *least* of *all* to a *feeling* I *killed* *first.*

nodes.speaker: Justiciar Vane
nodes.text: *(💀 You reach for the emotional appeal and, fumbling for the washerwoman's name Orla gave you, produce the wrong one — confidently calling her 'Helga' — and then, doubling down, invent a heart-rending detail about her sick mother that is, audibly, a fabrication. The Justiciar's expression flattens into pure professional contempt.)* …*Helga.* *(coldly)* There is no *Helga* on my *writ.* *(he taps the parchment)* There is a *Greta.* And *Greta's* *mother* has been *dead* these *ten* years — I *enforced* on *her*, *too.* *(he lifts the torch higher)* You come to *my* *door* to *plead* for souls whose *names* you *do* *not* *know*, with *details* you have *invented*, and you expect me to believe you *care* for them more than the *Choir* that at *least* *keeps* their *records* *straight?* *(grimly)* You have *insulted* them, friend, and *me, by* *lying* *badly* on their *behalf.* *(he gestures his men forward a step)* *Stand* *aside.* I will *not* *bargain* with a soul that cannot be *bothered* to *learn* the *names* of the *people* it's *dying* to *protect.* The *Choir*, at *least*, *spells* them *right* on the *way* to the *Wall.*

nodes.speaker: Justiciar Vane
nodes.text: *(The writ in his gauntlet goes still. A career man who lives by clean paper has just been told his paper is a forgery, and you watch the calculation race behind his eyes — not *is it true*, but *can I afford for it to be.*)* …*Venn.* *(quietly)* You know the *founding* *seal* by *name.* *(he lowers the torch a fraction)* I have *served* *writs* under that *Concord* for *six* years. I have *never* *once* *read* the *founding* *instrument* — *no* Justiciar *does*, it's *sealed*, it's *archived*, it's *above* our *station.* *(his jaw tightens)* But I *know* the *law* of *evidence*, friend, *better* than I know my *own* *name*, and the law of *evidence* says a *writ* is only as *sound* as the *instrument* it *cites* — and if the *instrument* is a *forgery*, then *every* writ *served* under it is *void*, and *every* *enforcer* who *served* one is *liable*, *retroactively*, *personally.* *(he is silent a long, cold moment)* …You understand what you have *handed* me. *(low)* Either you are *lying* — in which case *nothing* *changes* — or you are *right*, in which case I have spent *six* years committing *lawful* *atrocities* that were *never* *lawful*, and *so* has *every* Justiciar *alive*, and the *whole* *enforcement* *arm* of the *Choir* is *standing* on a *fraud.* *(he steps back from the door)* I *cannot* *risk* *serving* on it. *No* *career* *man* *could.* *(to his men)* *Withdraw.* Writ's *under* *legal* *cloud* — *founding* *instrument* *challenged.* We *serve* *nothing* till the *Choir's* own *advocates* *clear* it. *(low, to you)* You did not *save* these *souls* with *mercy*, Returned. You saved them with a *flaw* in a *title.* *(grimly)* It is the *only* *argument* the *Choir* has *ever* *respected.* They *built* their *Wall* on *paper.* *Paper*, it *seems*, is *also* how it *falls.*

nodes.speaker: Justiciar Vane
nodes.text: *(He stares at you through the slats — the cold pouring off you, the Wall's own chill worn by a soul that should be *in* it — and behind him, you hear it happen: the forty enforcers go *quiet*, the particular quiet of men who have just understood what they are looking at.)* …*Living* *proof.* *(barely)* That the *Wall* can be *escaped.* *(the torch trembles in his hand)* Do you know what you *are*, to a *Wall*-man, Returned? You are the *one* thing we are *never* *allowed* to *think*: that the *stone* is *not* *final.* That a *soul* can *go* *in* and *come* *out.* That *everything* we've *done* — every *child* we *dragged*, every *Greta* we *fed* to it — was *not* the *unbreakable* *law* of the *cosmos* but a *thing* *that* *could* *have* *been* *refused.* *(his voice cracks)* If you got *out*, then the *Wall* is not *justice.* It is just a *wall.* And we are not *enforcers* of *law.* We are *just* *men* who *put* *people* in *it.* *(behind him, an enforcer lowers his weapon. Then another.)* …I *cannot* give the *order.* *(barely)* *Look* at them, Returned — *look* at my *forty.* *Half* of them *can't* *meet* your *eyes*, and the *half* that *can* are *already* *lowering* their *arms*, because you *cannot* *drag* a *child* *past* the *proof* that the *dragging* was *never* *necessary* and *still* *believe* you're a *good* man *doing* a *hard* *duty.* *(he steps back from the door)* *Stand* *down.* We're — *(a long breath)* — we're *done* *here.* I'll not be the *Justiciar* who *harvested* a *room* with the *Returned* in the *doorway* and his *own* men *weeping.* *(low)* You *win* the *siege* by *standing* in it, cold one. The *Choir* never *did* learn to *counter* a *soul* that *proves* it *wrong.* *How* *could* they? They *built* the *whole* *order* on *pretending* you *can't* *exist.*

nodes.speaker: Justiciar Vane
nodes.text: *(He considers the demand with a frown — it is, infuriatingly, correct procedure — and a career man cannot refuse correct procedure without becoming the thing he prides himself on never being.)* …The *accused's* *right* to *hear* the *charge.* *(grudgingly)* In *full.* *(he cannot help the flicker of professional approval)* …It is *valid.* *Damn* it, it is *valid* — the *forms* *require* a *full* *recitation* if *demanded*, and *you* have *demanded* it, and a *service* that *skips* it is *appealable.* *(he draws himself up, unrolls the writ, and begins, because he *must*)* …*'In the matter of the Faithless sheltering at the almshouse of the Lamplit Quarter, by authority of the Founding Concord, the following souls are found forfeit…'* *(and he reads. Name by name. Clause by clause. Seal by seal — slowly, correctly, every word, while behind the barred door, beneath the sound of his own unhurried voice, the cellar hatch lifts and Orla begins to feed the room down the drain to the river, three souls a minute, children first.)* *(He reads for a long, long time. He is very thorough. By the time he reaches the final seal and looks up, expecting a barred door full of the doomed, he will find — though he does not know it yet — a room gone quiet and a hatch swinging on its hinge.)* …*'…so reckoned, so served.'* *(he rolls the writ)* There. *Now* *open*, or be *opened.* *(he does not yet know he has read an empty room its rights.)*

nodes.speaker: Justiciar Vane
nodes.text: *(He regards your silence through the slats, and sighs — the sigh of a man whose patience is a professional resource he is now choosing to spend.)* …*Nothing* for me. *(he hefts the torch)* They *all* say it, friend, from *behind* a *bar.* *'Nothing here, go away.'* *(grimly)* And then the *bar* *splinters*, and there is, in *fact*, a *room* full of the *listed*, and the *only* thing your *silence* *bought* them was a *harder* *entry* and a *grimmer* *form.* *(he signals his men; the booming on the door redoubles)* I gave you the *reasonable* *door*, Returned. You *chose* the *other* one. *(level, final)* I take *no* *pleasure* in this. I take *no* *anything* in this — that's *rather* the *point* of *me.* *(the wood groans)* *Last* *chance* to *speak* a *word* with *teeth* in it. *After* the *bar* *gives*, there are *no* *more* *words.* Only *forms.* And *forms*, friend, do not *negotiate.* They *file.*

variants.text: *(The thin woman lifts her head as the booming at the door stops, and the disbelief on her face is harder to watch than the fear was.)* …It *stopped.* *(barely)* The *fists.* The — *(she clutches the sleeping child)* …*you* did that. *(she searches your cold face)* I *heard* it — the *Justiciar's* voice, going *strange*, and then *quiet*, and then *boots* *leaving* instead of *coming* *in.* *(her arms loosen, fractionally, around her children)* I have been *waiting* for that *door* to *open* my *whole* *life*, traveler. *Every* *roof* I ever *slept* under, I *waited* for the *fists.* And *tonight* — *(her voice breaks, finally)* — tonight the *fists* *left.* I don't — I don't *know* how to *hold* a *night* where the *fists* *leave.* I've *no* *practice* at it. *Teach* me. *How* does a *Faithless* soul *breathe*, after the *door* *doesn't* *open?*

*(The thin woman watches you cross the room with the flat, conserving stillness of someone who has learned that hope is a thing you spend, and she has very little left.)* …You're *cold.* *(she does not flinch; her children flinch, and she stills them with a hand)* *Good.* The *warm* ones *promise.* The *warm* ones *promised* every *roof* before this *one*, and then the *fists* came and the warm ones were *gone.* *(she holds your gaze)* So be *cold*, traveler, and be *honest*, which is *colder*: are you *here* to *help* us, or to *count* us? *(the door booms)* Because I have *three* *children* and *no* *god* and a *door* that *won't* *hold*, and I have *no* *use* *left* for *anything* that *isn't* *true.* *Tell* me *true.* It's the *only* *coin* I'll *take.*

nodes.speaker: the Sheltered
nodes.text: *(She gathers her children closer and waits, rationing even her words, the way a soul does when it has learned that everything runs out.)* Ask, then. *(flatly)* Or *say* what you *came* to *say.* *(the door booms)* But *mind* — there's *little* *time*, and *less* of *me* to *spend* on it. I'm *saving* what's *left* for *them.* *(a glance at the children)* I *always* am.

choices.text: "What's your name? And theirs? I'm not going to fight for a list of 'the Faithless.' I'll fight for people, and people have names."
"You'll have a way out — Orla's seeing to it. When it's time, you take the children and you go, and you don't look back at the door. Can you do that? Run, not freeze?"

*(let the soul who walked out of the Wall tell her what waits if the door opens)* "Listen to me, because I'm the one person alive who can tell you true: I've been inside the Wall they want to take you to. It's not a death — it's a slow forgetting, awake, forever. I'd not let them take you to it for anything. But I also won't lie and say the door can't open. So here's the truth: I will spend everything I am keeping it shut, and you will keep your children ready to run, and between my cold and your quick feet, we will cheat that stone of all five of you."
"Hold on. Help's already moving. I'll be back before that door gives."

nodes.speaker: the Sheltered
nodes.text: *(Something flickers across her guarded face — surprise, that someone asked.)* …*Names.* *(she looks at you a long moment, deciding to spend the hope)* …*Maren.* I'm *Maren.* *(she touches each small head in turn)* *Pell* — no *relation* to the *clerk*, gods *forbid*, I *named* him before I *knew* there *was* one. *Sefa.* And the *baby's* *Wick* — for a *boy* my *eldest* *knew*, *under* a *bridge*, who — *(she stops)* — who *lived*, they *say. Someone* *cured* him. *(she searches your face)* …*you've* gone *still.* You *knew* him. The *bridge-boy.* *(a fragile, dawning thing)* Then it's *true*, what they say of you — that you *go* about *pulling* souls *out* of the *cold.* *(her arms loosen)* I *named* my *baby* after a *boy* who was *supposed* to *die* and *didn't.* *(barely)* I did it for *luck.* For *spite.* For — for *proof* that a *Faithless* child *can* *live.* *(she meets your eyes)* *Make* the *name* *true*, traveler. *All* *four* of them. *Maren*, *Pell*, *Sefa*, *Wick.* *Now* you *can't* *fight* for a *list.* Now you *have* to *fight* for *us.* That's — *(a cracked almost-smile)* — that's *why* we *give* the *fighters* our *names.* So they *can't* *put* us *down* *easy.*

nodes.speaker: the Sheltered
nodes.text: *(She looks down at her children, then back at you, and the flat stillness sharpens into something fiercer.)* …*Run*, not *freeze.* *(quietly)* You think I don't *know* the *difference?* *(she shifts the baby)* I've *run* from *four* *roofs*, traveler. I'm *good* at it — it's the *one* *skill* a *Faithless* *mother* *masters*, the *quick* *gathering*, the *not-looking-back.* *(grimly)* The *freezing* is for souls who still *think* the *door* might *not* *open.* I *gave* that up *years* ago. I *always* assume the *door* *opens.* It's *kept* us *alive.* *(she rises to a crouch, already gathering the little ones)* You tell *Orla* I'm *ready.* I've *been* *ready* my *whole* *life.* Just — *(her composure cracks, one fraction)* — just *don't* let the *way* *out* be *another* *lie*, traveler. I can *run* my *children* *anywhere.* I *can't* run them *somewhere* that *isn't* *there.* *(she steadies)* *Tell* me the *drain* is *real*, and *point* me at it, and we are *gone* like we were *never* *here.* *Gone* is the *one* thing we're *better* at than the *Choir.*

nodes.speaker: the Sheltered
nodes.text: *(She listens — really listens, the way she clearly hasn't let herself listen to anyone in years — and when you finish, there are tears standing in her eyes, but her voice is steady.)* …You've *been* *in* *it.* *(barely)* And you came *back* to *stand* in a *doorway* for *strangers.* *(she wipes her eyes, fierce)* That's — *no* one's ever told me the *truth* of the *Wall* before, traveler. The *Choir* says *'forfeit'*, like it's *clean.* The *warm* ones say *'don't* *worry'*, like it's *nothing.* And *you* — *you* say it's a *slow* *forgetting*, *awake*, *forever*, and *then* you say *you'll* *spend* *everything* to keep us *from* it. *(she pulls her children in)* *That's* the *first* *honest* *hope* anyone's *ever* *handed* me. *Not* *'you're* *safe.'* *'I'll* *fight*, and *you* *run*, and *together* we *cheat* it.'* *(she nods, hard, and there's iron in it now)* *That* I can *hold.* That I can *teach* the *children* — not *'don't* *be* *afraid'*, but *'be* *afraid*, and *run* *anyway*, because the *cold* one is *buying* us the *seconds.'* *(she meets your eyes)* *Buy* them, traveler. *Every* *second* you *hold* that *door* is a *child* *down* the *drain* and *into* the *dark* and *free.* We'll *do* *our* *part.* *Cheat* the *stone* of *Wick's* *namesake.* *Cheat* it of *all* of us. *I* *believe* a *cold* *thing* can. I've *seen* what the *warm* ones *can't* *do.* Maybe it *takes* the *cold.*

nodes.speaker: the Sheltered
nodes.text: *(She nods, once, and pulls her children close, and watches you turn toward the door with an expression caught between dread and the unfamiliar, dangerous beginning of trust.)* …*Before* it *gives.* *(quietly)* You *said* *before* it *gives.* *(she files the promise away with all the others, but this one, you can see, she *wants* to believe)* I'll *hold* you to it, *cold* one. *(a glance at the door, then back)* And *traveler* — *(her voice drops, fierce and low)* — if it *does* *give*, and you *can't* *keep* it — *don't* you *die* in that *doorway* for *us.* You go about *pulling* souls from the *cold*; that's *worth* more than *four* of us and a *baby.* *(her jaw sets)* *Run*, if it *comes* to *running.* I'll not have the *one* soul who *tells* the *truth* *spent* on a *door* that was *always* going to *open.* *(she steadies)* …But *don't* *tell* me you *will*. Just — *go.* *Hold* it as *long* as it *holds.* And *come* *back*, like you *said.* *Before* it *gives.* I'm *choosing*, *tonight*, to *believe* a *cold* thing *keeps* its *word.* *Don't* make me *wrong.* I've *so* *little* *left* to *be* *wrong* *with.*
